_meta.spine_indices_covered: 29-57 (29 businesses, the LAST 29 of the CNC machine shop spine)
_meta.method: WebFetch of About/History pages where reachable + targeted WebSearch for owner identification + cross-reference to spine independence_notes. Entity-status verification deferred to Comptroller spot-check phase (most rows already noted 'Active' status implicit from spine; PE-rollup exclusions flagged here).
_meta.schema_notes: Same field set as garage_door_enrich_batch_1 / painting_contractor_enrich_batch_1, adapted to CNC vertical: estimated_truck_count → not_applicable (replaced with estimated_machine_count); has_maintenance_plan → not_applicable (replaced with cad_cam_software_in_use); ida_member → not_applicable (replaced with ntma_member and as9100_certified).
critical_exclusions_discovered_this_batc: Owens Machine and Tool (spine_idx 45) — acquired by Precision Aerospace Holdings May 2024 (PE roll-up, same parent as Decatur Machine Services and Applegate EDM). EXCLUDE from target pool.
Icon Machine Company (spine_idx 50) — acquired by Precision Aerospace Holdings August 2022. EXCLUDE from target pool.

Neo Industries (spine_idx 52) — recent ownership transition in 2019 (Jerry Simmons CEO, Obie D Day President; original founder Bill Horn retired 2006). Not a coasting solo-owner — FLAG as recent acquisition, not 12-36 month seller.
Texas Contract Manufacturing Group (spine_idx 37) — multi-subsidiary group (already marked EXCLUDED in spine). Confirm exclusion.
C & M Machining LP — Sugar Land branch (spine_idx 28) — same legal entity as Navasota C & M (spine_idx 23 in batch_1). DEDUPLICATE.
B&R Productions — San Antonio service branch (spine_idx 42) — same legal entity as New Waverly B&R (spine_idx 21 in batch_1). DEDUPLICATE.
high_conviction_a_tier_from_this_batch: Manda Machine Company (spine_idx 35) — 74-yr tenure, 3rd-gen Ellard brothers running, ISO+AS9100, Dallas
Cameron Machine Shop (spine_idx 34) — 1967 founding, 3rd-gen Christi Cameron 2007 succession, 25k sqft, woman-owned
Bates Machine & Mfg (spine_idx 29) — founder Earl Bates Jr deceased 2019, 2nd/3rd gen Paul Bates + Mary Kaye Wilson now operating, 3-gen status proven
Continental Manufacturing (spine_idx 32) — 3rd-gen (Doc Eldon→David Ward→Judd Stretcher), 70+ yr tenure, but verify size (parent Continental NH3 is family-controlled)
Halsey Manufacturing (spine_idx 31) — Don Halsey founder 1979, no public successor named, 45+ yrs

owner_name: Paul Bates & Mary Kaye Wilson (2nd gen, children of founder Earl Bates Jr. who lived 1922-2019)
service_mix: diversified (aerospace, medical, oil&gas, automotive, defense, ag, construction, renewables)
coasting_tells: Founder Earl Bates Jr passed away 2019; succession transition implicit
Family-owned & operated since 1975 — 51-yr tenure
Brochure-style website with no online quoting; phone-first intake (972) 782-7351
No CAD/CAM software disclosed on website
No certifications disclosed on website (no ISO 9001, no AS9100, no ITAR)
Farmersville (rural Collin county, NE of McKinney) — off main metro = less PE consolidator attention
Marketing copy emphasizes '3-generation machine shop service experience' — soft-sell, not growth-oriented
Three-paragraph site with limited capabilities depth = classic small/coasting tell

successor_indicators.verified_via: Earl Bates Jr obituary (Turrentine-Jackson-Morrow funeral home, 2019) — survived by Paul Bates and Mary Kaye Wilson, both of Farmersville TX, both presumed active in company per company self-description of '3-generation experience'

high_conviction_signals: 51-yr tenure (founded 1975)
Founder deceased 2019 — 2nd gen now owns, ~6 yrs into transition window
Sibling co-owners (Paul + Mary Kaye) = potential exit catalyst when one wants to retire / cash out
Rural Farmersville = off-PE radar
No external certifications (ISO/AS) = anti-PE-roll-up tell (PE acquirers usually drive certifications)
notes: Strong A-tier candidate. Diversified industry mix (11 industries) suggests recession-resistant book. Validate cash health via Texas Workforce employer reports or DataAxle.

successor_indicators.verified_via: Continental NH3 Products company history page — 'currently run by their 3rd generation of family that is as passionate as their original proprietor, their grandfather Doc Eldon and their father David Ward.' Judd Stretcher named as current President of NH3 parent.

high_conviction_signals: 72-yr tenure (founded 1954) — top of cohort

Add to scorer as flag-for-special-structure.
